title: Unit Economics Dashboard v1 launch checklist + phased rollout plan
preview:
Goal
Ship the internal Unit Economics Dashboard v1 safely with explicit quality gates, SLO readiness, access controls, and a phased rollout plan aligned with Finance signoff.

Scope (this ticket)
- Consolidate the v1 launch checklist into a single source of truth for go/no-go.
- Define the phased rollout (dogfood -> Finance/Product -> broader Eng -> exec KPI consumption) including comms and support expectations.
- Define launch gates that are objectively verifiable (queries, dashboards, alerts, reconciliation report).

Non-goals
- Building remaining pipeline/UI work (tracked in dependencies).

Links
- PRD: sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/internal-unit-economics-dashboard-prd.json
- Metric dictionary: sources/confluence/eng-platform/dashboards-and-alerts/unit-economics-dashboard-metrics-and-definitions.json
- Pipeline SLOs: sources/confluence/eng-sre/slo-and-error-budgets/unit-economics-pipeline-slo.json
- Oncall guide: sources/confluence/eng-platform/how-tos/oncall-guide-unit-economics-dashboard.json
- Finance methodology: sources/confluence/finance-and-legal/budgeting-and-forecasting/blended-cost-methodology-v1.json
- Feature flag/RBAC: ENG-8434 / PR sources/github/redwood/pr-19397-feature-flag-and-rbac-for-internal-dashboard.json
- Reconciliation report: ENG-8430 / PR sources/github/redwood/pr-19390-reconciliation-report-vs-billing-totals.json

Owners / approvers
- SRE readiness + alerts routing: Sean Gallagher (owner), Allison Grant (review)
- Data quality checks: Caleb Johnson (review)
- Telemetry correctness (output tokens / streaming parity): Nadia Rahman (review)
- Warehouse views correctness/perf: Mei Lin (review)
- Access controls/audit: Kaitlyn Nguyen + Marcus Lin (review)
- Finance signoff: Laura Bennett + Rishi Malhotra
- Product signoff: Marissa Cole

Launch checklist (go/no-go gates)
1) Metric definition + Finance signoff
- Gate: Finance confirms the dashboard definition of “blended $/1M output tokens” matches methodology v1.
- Evidence:
  - Signed-off methodology doc updated with final assumptions (idle allocation, overhead inclusion rules).
  - Reconciliation report is available and reviewed for at least 7 consecutive days (see section 5).
- Owner: Rishi Malhotra
- Notes: Any changes to rate card versioning must be called out explicitly in the dashboard (rate_card_version label) and in the exec readout notes.

2) Data completeness + dimension coverage
- Gate: Hourly aggregates present for last 72 hours with dimension coverage above thresholds.
- Evidence (from pipeline SLO dashboard + validation queries):
  - Completeness: >= 99.5% of expected hourly partitions available (per region) over trailing 72h.
  - Missing dimension rates (trailing 24h):
    - region = UNKNOWN <= 0.5% of output tokens
    - customer_tier = UNKNOWN <= 0.5% of output tokens
    - model_id = UNKNOWN <= 0.1% of output tokens
    - quantization_profile = UNKNOWN allowed up to 5% for v1 (known gap for older runtime versions), but must be stable (no step change > 2x day-over-day).
- Owner: Caleb Johnson
- Notes: If UNKNOWN quantization_profile is above 5% for a new deploy, treat as regression and block rollout expansion beyond allowlist.

3) Telemetry correctness (output tokens parity)
- Gate: Streaming and non-streaming paths produce equivalent output_token_count within tolerance.
- Evidence:
  - Canary check green for 7 days: sources/github/perf-canary/pr-455-add-canary-check-for-output-token-metering.json
  - Regression tests merged: ENG-8437
  - Spot-check query: For a sample of requests with both streaming/non-streaming routes, distribution of output tokens matches within +/- 1 token median difference and no systematic undercount.
- Owner: Nadia Rahman
- Notes: Tool calls / function calling responses must be included in output token counts (exclude input/prompt tokens from the denominator for the primary blended metric).

4) SLOs + alerting + runbook links
- Gate: SLO dashboard exists, alerts are enabled, paging policy agreed, and runbooks are linked in alert payloads.
- Evidence:
  - SLO targets configured and reviewed:
    - Freshness: hourly aggregates available within 90 minutes of the hour (99% in 7d)
    - Correctness/completeness: see dimension coverage thresholds above
  - Alert routes:
    - Page: Eng SRE oncall (primary) for pipeline freshness/completeness violations
    - Notify (no page): #eng-platform, #finance (for prolonged discrepancies > 4h)
  - Runbook links present in alerts: sources/github/redwood/pr-19452-oncall-runbook-links-in-alert-payloads.json
- Owner: Sean Gallagher
- Notes: Tune alert noise before expanding to Finance default usage; Finance should not be the first detector of pipeline issues.

5) Reconciliation vs billing / infra spend
- Gate: Reconciliation report exists, has known limitations documented, and is within expected variance.
- Evidence:
  - Report available in warehouse + Console internal link: ENG-8430
  - Trailing 7-day reconciliation within tolerance (agreed with Finance):
    - Hosted: blended cost total within +/- 3% of finance-allocated infra totals for the same window, after applying known exclusions.
    - Dedicated/Private: within +/- 5% (due to capacity reservation allocation timing), with a documented explanation if outside.
- Owner: Cole Summers (integrations) + Rishi Malhotra
- Notes: If variance exceeds tolerance for >24h, block rollout expansion and open incident (SEV-2) with Finance notified.

6) Access controls (internal-only) + auditability
- Gate: Feature flag is enabled only for allowlist; RBAC roles set; audit logs for access are on.
- Evidence:
  - Feature flag: internal_unit_economics_dashboard (default OFF)
  - RBAC policy: only employees in roles “finance”, “product”, “engineering” with explicit allowlist during v1.
  - Audit logging: access events recorded (viewer, timestamp, route, query type), retention 30 days.
- Owner: Kaitlyn Nguyen
- Security review: Marcus Lin
- Notes: UI must not show customer identifiers in top-N tables; only aggregated views allowed (see privacy thread: sources/slack/eng-platform/1738362010-topn-tables-customer-id-privacy.json).

7) Performance + warehouse safety
- Gate: Dashboard queries meet latency targets and caching TTLs are aligned with freshness expectations.
- Evidence:
  - P95 query API latency <= 2.0s for default views (last 7 days, group_by=model) and <= 4.0s for worst-case (group_by=model_version + region).
  - API caching enabled with separate “live” vs “finance” views (per decision in sources/slack/eng-platform/1738200021-api-caching-vs-freshness.json)
  - Backfill throttling plan validated (warehouse health maintained during backfills).
- Owner: Chloe Martin + Mei Lin

8) Comms + support plan
- Gate: Announcement drafted, feedback channel established, and an escalation path for Finance questions exists.
- Evidence:
  - Internal announcement in #general + #finance + #eng-platform with link + short caveats.
  - Dedicated Slack thread for feedback: #eng-platform (tag @Felix Schneider for UI bugs, @Caleb Johnson for data issues).
  - “How to interpret” blurb embedded in UI (link to metric dictionary).
- Owner: Talia Benson + Marissa Cole

Phased rollout plan
Phase 0 — Pre-flight (now through go/no-go)
- Run checklist items 1–7, verify alerting and reconciliation stability.
- Limit access to core build team (Console + Telemetry + SRE + Finance reviewers).

Phase 1 — Engineering dogfood (3–5 business days)
- Expand allowlist to Console/Telemetry/Runtime/Infra engineering managers and oncall.
- Collect feedback focused on:
  - Filter correctness (region/tier/model)
  - Drilldown consistency and performance
  - Confusing “blended” vs “marginal” interpretation
- Success criteria: <3 P1 bugs filed; no SLO violations; no reconciliation variance > tolerance.

Phase 2 — Finance + Product enablement (1–2 weeks)
- Add Finance and Product allowlist (FP&A + Console/Optimize PMs) and schedule 30-min walkthrough.
- Create saved views defaults for Finance (QTD and trailing 7d), aligned to Finance timezone/day boundary.
- Success criteria: Finance confirms they can self-serve QTD views; signoff blockers resolved (see finance thread: sources/slack/finance/1738720044-finance-signoff-blockers.json).

Phase 3 — Broader internal rollout
- Remove explicit allowlist requirement, keep internal-only RBAC and audit logging.
- Publish FAQ and link runbook + metric definitions prominently.
- Ongoing: weekly review of data quality dashboard and top regressions.

Go/No-Go meeting checklist (30 min)
- Attendees: Sean Gallagher, Chloe Martin, Logan Wright, Rishi Malhotra, Marissa Cole, Kaitlyn Nguyen, Marcus Lin
- Agenda:
  - SLO dashboard status (freshness/completeness)
  - Reconciliation report variance (7d)
  - Access control readiness (flag + RBAC + audit)
  - Known limitations + messaging (what not to claim in exec readout)
  - Decision + next rollout phase date

Open questions
- Do we require quantization_profile coverage >= 95% before Phase 2, or is 90% acceptable with a confidence indicator? (Proposal: >=95% for Finance Phase 2.)
- Which timezone is authoritative for Finance “day” in the UI (PST vs UTC)? Current proposal: PST for Finance saved views; keep UTC for pipeline partitions; document the mapping.

Risks / mitigations
- Risk: Backfills overload warehouse and degrade dashboard latency.
  - Mitigation: throttle backfills, run in maintenance windows, and add warehouse load guardrails (reference: sources/slack/eng-sre/1738726666-backfill-job-overloaded-warehouse.json).
- Risk: Users misinterpret blended as marginal and chase noise.
  - Mitigation: visualization guidelines + confidence indicator + in-product explainer.

Acceptance criteria
- Checklist above is finalized, reviewed by SRE + Finance + Security, and used as the go/no-go artifact.
- Rollout phases are scheduled with owners and comms plan.
- All gates have a concrete verification method (dashboard or query) and a named owner.

title: E2E test suite for Hosted data residency region pinning (blocking, streaming, fallback)
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocks) for Enterprise customers. Support and Sales escalations show the highest-risk regressions are:
- Region-pinned orgs accidentally routed to a disallowed region via endpoint selection or fallback.
- Streaming endpoints returning generic 500 / truncated frames instead of the standardized residency violation error.
- Cross-region fallback attempts when a region is degraded/capacity constrained.

This ticket adds an end-to-end test suite that runs against staging (and optionally prod-canary) to continuously validate residency enforcement behavior across routing + gateway + streaming.

## Goals
- Catch regressions in the full request path (gateway → policy eval → routing → response formatting).
- Validate error contract for blocked requests is consistent across streaming and non-streaming.
- Validate fallback behavior respects allowed regions and never crosses region boundaries by default.
- Provide a CI gating plan that’s strict enough to prevent shipping regressions but pragmatic about flake.

## Out of scope
- Deep verification of internal log/audit sinks (we’ll assert response-level signals and rely on unit/integration tests for internal telemetry schema).
- Multi-region replication scenarios (non-goal for initial release).

## Test environments
- Primary: staging (feature flag enabled, seeded test orgs).
- Optional: prod-canary (once allowlisted) — smoke only, no destructive config mutations.

## Owners / reviewers
- SRE: Sean Gallagher (assignee)
- API Gateway: Amara Diallo / Brian Murphy
- Routing: Mina Haddad / Ravi Menon
- Control plane: Ethan Park

## Notes on seeded test data
We will maintain 2–3 dedicated e2e orgs in staging with stable IDs and short-lived API keys created during test setup:
- org_e2e_residency_eu_only: allowed_regions=["eu-west-1"], primary_region="eu-west-1"
- org_e2e_residency_eu_multi: allowed_regions=["eu-west-1","eu-central-1"], primary_region="eu-west-1"
- org_e2e_residency_unpinned: no residency policy (control)

Policies are created/updated via Control Plane Admin API in setup, and cleaned up in teardown (best-effort).

title: Oncall runbook + playbook docs for Unit Economics dashboard/pipeline (link from alerts)
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out the internal Unit Economics dashboard and hourly pipeline. For exec/Finance reporting, we need predictable operational response when freshness/completeness or reconciliation drifts. Alerts are being added (see pipeline SLO + completeness checks), but oncall currently lacks a single, decision-tree style playbook.

This ticket creates the oncall runbook pages and ensures *every* pipeline/dashboard alert includes a direct link to the relevant section.

## Goals
- Provide a single “start here” runbook for Unit Economics pipeline + dashboard discrepancies.
- Add decision trees for the most common failure modes: missing hours, output token undercount (esp streaming), missing dimensions (region/tier/model/quant_profile), cost attribution/rate-card issues, and reconciliation mismatches vs billing.
- Ensure alert payloads link to the runbook and the exact remediation section (deep link / anchor).
- Make it safe for Eng Oncall to take first action, with clear escalation points to Telemetry/Analytics + Finance.

## Non-goals
- Creating new alert rules (covered in ENG-8429 / observability-pack PRs).
- Changing metric definitions or cost attribution logic (ADR-owned).

## Deliverables
1) Confluence runbook pages (internal):
   - “Unit Economics Oncall Guide” (start page)
   - “Decision tree: missing data / freshness lag”
   - “Decision tree: token metering regressions (streaming parity)”
   - “Decision tree: dimension coverage regressions (region/tier/model/quant_profile)”
   - “Decision tree: reconciliation mismatch vs billing”
   - “Backfill procedure + safety guardrails”

2) Alert payload linking:
   - Update alert annotations (Grafana/Prometheus or equivalent) to include:
     - Runbook URL
     - ‘What changed?’ dashboards/queries link
     - Owner + escalation channel

## Runbook structure (template)
Each section should include:
- **What this means** (impact on dashboard correctness / Finance reporting)
- **How to confirm** (specific dashboards + warehouse queries)
- **Likely causes** (ranked)
- **Immediate mitigation** (stop-the-bleed actions)
- **Fix forward** (what to patch / who owns)
- **Backfill** (when safe, how to throttle)
- **Escalation** (who, when)
- **Post-incident notes** (what to capture for retro)

## Decision trees (v1)
### A) Alert: Data freshness SLO breach (hourly rollup missing)
1. Check ingestion lag vs aggregation lag.
   - If ingestion behind: confirm upstream metering pipeline health; pause backfills if running.
   - If ingestion OK but aggregates missing: inspect hourly job status / recent deploys.
2. Confirm whether missing hours are isolated to a region/model/tier.
   - If isolated: likely dimension mapping or null-handling drop.
   - If global: likely job failure, warehouse timeout, or schema change.
3. Mitigation:
   - Re-run failed hour(s) idempotently.
   - If warehouse overloaded: throttle batch size, move to maintenance window.
4. Escalate:
   - Telemetry/Analytics (Logan Wright / Mei Lin) if aggregation job or warehouse.
   - SRE (Sean Gallagher) if infra incident.

### B) Alert: Output tokens drop / undercount (streaming parity)
1. Compare streaming vs non-streaming output token totals for same route/model.
2. Validate gateway/runtime emits output token events for streaming chunk paths.
3. Mitigation:
   - Roll back the suspected change or enable canary gate.
   - Run parity validation query against last known good commit window.
4. Escalate to Nadia Rahman (token metering lead) if mismatch persists beyond single deploy.

### C) Alert: Dimension coverage regression (region/tier/quant_profile)
1. Identify missing dimension(s):
   - region_normalized nulls
   - customer_tier unknown
   - model_identifier empty/aliased unexpectedly
   - quantization_profile value rejected / cardinality guardrail triggered
2. Determine if drop started at deploy boundary.
3. Mitigation:
   - Hotfix mapping table / null-handling
   - Temporarily route unknowns to ‘unknown’ bucket (do NOT drop rows)
4. Escalate:
   - Region mapping: Infra/Telemetry
   - Tier mapping: Platform billing metadata owner + Kaitlyn Nguyen for authz-related changes
   - Quant profile: Quentin Harris (model metadata) + Applied ML

### D) Alert: Reconciliation mismatch vs billing totals
1. Confirm time window + timezone boundaries align with Finance reporting.
2. Confirm rate card version and allocation cadence used in the pipeline.
3. Check for exclusion rules (internal traffic, free credits, test orgs) causing expected deltas.
4. Mitigation:
   - If the mismatch is > threshold and unexplained: freeze Finance-facing view and publish a known-issue note.
5. Escalate to Rishi Malhotra (FP&A) for methodology disputes; Laura Bennett for exec comms if KPI impacted.

## Links (artifacts)
- SLO doc: sources/confluence/eng-sre/slo-and-error-budgets/unit-economics-pipeline-slo.json
- Existing draft guide: sources/confluence/eng-platform/how-tos/oncall-guide-unit-economics-dashboard.json
- Metric dictionary: sources/confluence/eng-platform/dashboards-and-alerts/unit-economics-dashboard-metrics-and-definitions.json
- ADR (cost attribution): sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-034-unit-economics-cost-attribution-model.json

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Runbook pages exist and are linked from the main Unit Economics dashboard page.
- [x] Every v1 alert has a runbook URL in the payload (deep link preferred).
- [x] Runbook includes at least one ‘first 5 minutes’ checklist per failure mode.
- [x] Backfill section includes throttling guidance + verification queries.
- [x] Escalation matrix includes Finance sign-off requirements for methodology/reconciliation alerts.

## Implementation notes / changes
- Add runbook link annotation to alert rules generated by observability pack.
- Include runbook link in Slack alert routing messages (where applicable).

## Updates
- 2025-02-03 (Aisha): Drafted decision trees for freshness + token undercount; request review from Nadia + Mei.
- 2025-02-07 (Mei): Added warehouse verification queries; noted partitioning-related false positives during backfill.
- 2025-02-10 (Nadia): Updated streaming parity checklist to include tool-calls + truncation edge cases.
- 2025-02-14 (Sean): Confirmed escalation matrix + Finance comms path; ready to link from alerts.
- 2025-02-18 (Aisha): Landed runbook links in alert payloads; marked done.

## PRs / follow-ups
- Related: sources/github/redwood/pr-19452-oncall-runbook-links-in-alert-payloads.json
- Follow-up (nice-to-have): Add a ‘confidence indicator’ section in runbook once metric coverage scoring ships.

title: Automate periodic restore drill in staging for Redwood Private and report results to Slack/on-call
preview:
Goal
Automate a repeatable restore drill for a Redwood Private staging environment, on a fixed cadence, using the same supported backup artifacts + restore workflow customers will use. Report drill outcomes (success/failure + key timings) to Slack and wire alerting consistent with SRE paging policy.

Why
We’ve been doing ad-hoc restore tests and they keep drifting from “customer-realistic” procedures. We need a predictable signal that:
1) backups are usable,
2) restore remains deterministic across platform versions,
3) validation checks catch the most common restore regressions (ordering, missing CRDs, bad Helm values, auth/routing config not restored).

Scope
In scope:
- A scheduled restore drill (weekly by default) in staging.
- Uses the Private installer restore workflow (restore apply) against the most recent valid control-plane backup artifact.
- Runs post-restore validation (smoke tests + basic SLO checks) and produces a machine-readable report.
- Sends a Slack summary to #eng-oncall and #eng-sre, with links to logs/dashboards.
- Emits metrics for drill success + duration; integrate with existing dashboards/alerts (ENG-9826).

Out of scope (for this ticket):
- True region-loss simulation (handled by DR game day plan / runbooks).
- Full etcd-level cluster recovery (covered in infra runbook; separate workflow).
- Customer-facing docs (tracked in ENG-9828).

Proposed design
High-level flow:
1) Select backup artifact
   - Prefer the latest backup marked VERIFIED (manifest integrity + decrypt test + checksum match).
   - Backup source should mirror what we recommend: S3 (staging bucket) w/ KMS or local object-store equivalent.
   - Hard requirement: do NOT reuse “partial” backups from an in-progress job.

2) Create isolated restore target
   - Restore into a clean staging target to avoid the “it worked because state already existed” problem.
   - Option A (preferred): provision an ephemeral K8s cluster in staging (kOps/EKS sandbox) and tear down after.
   - Option B: dedicated restore namespace + dedicated release name, with strict safeguards to prevent overwriting the primary staging control plane.
   - Safeguards:
     - Require explicit --restore-target-id=drill-<timestamp>
     - Admission guard: block restores unless DRILL_MODE=true
     - Block restore if it detects production-like cluster tags

3) Restore
   - Run: redwood-private-installer restore apply --backup-uri <…> --target <…> --noninteractive
   - Capture full logs + validation report artifact.

4) Validate
   - Run a standard validation suite:
     - Kubernetes readiness: expected Deployments/StatefulSets ready
     - Control plane health endpoint(s) green
     - Tenant config present (at least 1 known staging tenant)
     - Routing policies restored (canary/fallback config exists)
     - Basic API operation (smoke: create a small inference request against a staging model route)
   - Produce a JSON report (schema: {status, started_at, finished_at, restore_duration_s, validation_duration_s, failing_checks[], artifact_version, platform_version})

5) Report
   - Slack message with:
     - Overall result + durations
     - Backup artifact ID + format version
     - Platform version
     - Link to logs (CI job or internal log UI)
     - Link to dashboard panel (restore drill history)
   - Paging policy (align with #eng-sre thread):
     - Staging: page only after 2 consecutive failures OR failure persists > 24h
     - Always notify Slack on first failure
     - Suppress during scheduled maintenance window (configurable)

Implementation plan
- Add a “restore drill” CI workflow (GitHub Actions or Buildkite, whichever is standard for private-installer pipelines) triggered weekly + manual dispatch.
- Add a small orchestrator script (bash/python) that:
  - fetches latest VERIFIED backup manifest
  - stands up target (cluster or namespace)
  - runs restore apply
  - runs validations
  - pushes metrics
  - posts Slack
  - cleans up
- Store drill artifacts:
  - validation report JSON
  - installer logs
  - k8s events dump (on failure)

Acceptance criteria
- A scheduled restore drill runs at least weekly in staging without manual intervention.
- Drill uses the supported restore workflow (no hidden shortcuts).
- Success path posts a Slack message with timings + artifact metadata.
- Failure path posts Slack with failing checks + links to debug artifacts.
- Metrics available:
  - private_restore_drill_success{env="staging"}
  - private_restore_drill_duration_seconds{phase="restore|validate"}
  - private_restore_drill_last_success_timestamp
- Alerting rule exists and matches SRE paging policy (notify on first failure; page after repeat failures).
- Drill is safe: cannot run against production clusters and cannot overwrite the primary staging control plane.

Risks / open questions
- Isolation strategy: ephemeral cluster is closest to real DR but may increase infra cost + flakiness. If we do namespace-only, we must be very strict about not accidentally restoring into the main release.
- Backup selection: we need an unambiguous “VERIFIED” marker. If not available yet, we’ll implement a lightweight verification step (decrypt + checksum) before restore.
- Validation scope: keep minimal to avoid false negatives, but include at least one end-to-end inference smoke test.

Related links
- PR (restore workflow): sources/github/redwood-private-installer/pr-489-add-restore-apply-workflow-and-validation.json
- PR (backup create): sources/github/redwood-private-installer/pr-482-add-backup-create-command.json
- PR (metrics/tracing): sources/github/redwood/pr-24344-emit-metrics-for-backup-and-restore-operations.json
- Linear (metrics/alerts): ENG-9826
- Linear (restore CLI): ENG-9822

Progress updates
2025-02-03 (Sean Gallagher)
- Drafted initial workflow outline for weekly staging drill. Waiting on confirmation of where “VERIFIED” backup manifest will live (installer output vs control-plane status endpoint).

2025-02-10 (Sean Gallagher)
- Implemented prototype script in staging: selects latest backup by timestamp, runs restore apply into dedicated namespace, runs minimal health checks.
- Observed failure mode: missing CRDs due to ordering when running restore into a namespace-only target. Likely need preflight to ensure CRDs/cluster-scoped resources installed before restore (ties to Helm hooks / chart restore template).

2025-02-14 (Rafael Mendes)
- DR rehearsal notes indicate we need stricter cleanup and better failure artifact capture (k8s events + Helm history). Adding to plan.

Next steps
- Align with Sergio Costa on metric names + dashboard panel.
- Add production-safety guardrail: refuse to run if cluster has label environment=prod or if context name matches prod patterns.
- Decide isolation approach (ephemeral cluster vs namespace) by EOW; if ephemeral cluster is blocked, proceed with namespace + strict guardrails and document limitations.

Owner(s)
- Primary: Sean Gallagher (SRE)
- Consult: Rafael Mendes (game day), Sergio Costa (observability), Ethan Park (restore workflow edge cases)

Definition of done
- Workflow merged, scheduled, and successfully completes at least 2 consecutive weekly runs.
- Alerting tested (Slack notify on injected failure; page only per policy).
- Runbook snippet added for on-call: “how to triage a failed restore drill” (link to eng-sre runbook ticket if needed).

title: Execute Private DR game day + write-up and follow-up action plan
preview:
Goal
- Run a realistic disaster recovery (DR) game day for Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem assumptions), validate backup/restore runbooks + automation, and produce an actionable follow-up plan.

Why
- Enterprise prospects (FinBank, MedData) are explicitly asking for evidence of repeatable DR procedures and RPO/RTO attainment. This game day is the internal bar for whether we can confidently claim “supported + documented + repeatable” recovery.

Scope for this game day
- Primary: Control-plane restore into a clean Kubernetes cluster using the new installer workflows (backup create / restore apply) + documented runbooks.
- Secondary: Validate observability and on-call readiness (dashboards/alerts, failure modes, escalation criteria).
- Out of scope: Full etcd cluster restore as the primary path (covered by infra runbook as last resort), and data-plane “stateful” restoration (data-plane should be stateless + redeployable; ensure config required for deterministic redeploy is captured).

Scenario (selected)
- “Cluster corruption / control-plane loss” in single-region VPC deployment.
- Constraints: no outbound internet during restore steps except to the configured backup storage endpoint (simulate restricted egress posture). Simulate IAM least-privilege role usage.

Participants (roles)
- Incident commander: Rafael Mendes
- Ops lead / executor: Sean Gallagher
- Control plane engineering support: Ethan Park / Benji Okafor
- Observability: Sergio Costa
- Security/crypto reviewer (observer): Hanae Suzuki
- PM/notes: Talia Benson

Pre-reads / artifacts
- Game day plan: sources/confluence/oncall-and-incident-response/game-days/private-dr-gameday-plan.json
- Prior rehearsal notes: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/site-reliability-engineering/dr-gameday-notes-2025-02-14.json
- Restore validation test plan: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/private-deployments/restore-validation-test-plan.json

Success criteria
- Execute restore end-to-end with runbooks only (no tribal knowledge), capture timestamps for RTO.
- Demonstrate we can meet target RPO (last successful backup age within target) and can validate integrity.
- Produce a write-up with: timeline, what broke, root causes, mitigations/workarounds, and a prioritized action plan (owners + deadlines).

Deliverables
- Confluence/Drive write-up link in this ticket.
- List of follow-up Linear issues (existing or newly filed), tagged and prioritized.

Notes

text:
Goal
- Run a realistic disaster recovery (DR) game day for Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem assumptions), validate backup/restore runbooks + automation, and produce an actionable follow-up plan.

Why
- Enterprise prospects (FinBank, MedData) are explicitly asking for evidence of repeatable DR procedures and RPO/RTO attainment. This game day is the internal bar for whether we can confidently claim “supported + documented + repeatable” recovery.

Scope for this game day
- Primary: Control-plane restore into a clean Kubernetes cluster using the new installer workflows (backup create / restore apply) + documented runbooks.
- Secondary: Validate observability and on-call readiness (dashboards/alerts, failure modes, escalation criteria).
- Out of scope: Full etcd cluster restore as the primary path (covered by infra runbook as last resort), and data-plane “stateful” restoration (data-plane should be stateless + redeployable; ensure config required for deterministic redeploy is captured).

Scenario (selected)
- “Cluster corruption / control-plane loss” in single-region VPC deployment.
- Constraints: no outbound internet during restore steps except to the configured backup storage endpoint (simulate restricted egress posture). Simulate IAM least-privilege role usage.

Participants (roles)
- Incident commander: Rafael Mendes
- Ops lead / executor: Sean Gallagher
- Control plane engineering support: Ethan Park / Benji Okafor
- Observability: Sergio Costa
- Security/crypto reviewer (observer): Hanae Suzuki
- PM/notes: Talia Benson

Pre-reads / artifacts
- Game day plan: sources/confluence/oncall-and-incident-response/game-days/private-dr-gameday-plan.json
- Prior rehearsal notes: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/site-reliability-engineering/dr-gameday-notes-2025-02-14.json
- Restore validation test plan: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/private-deployments/restore-validation-test-plan.json

Success criteria
- Execute restore end-to-end with runbooks only (no tribal knowledge), capture timestamps for RTO.
- Demonstrate we can meet target RPO (last successful backup age within target) and can validate integrity.
- Produce a write-up with: timeline, what broke, root causes, mitigations/workarounds, and a prioritized action plan (owners + deadlines).

Deliverables
- Confluence/Drive write-up link in this ticket.
- List of follow-up Linear issues (existing or newly filed), tagged and prioritized.

Notes
- Expectation is not “perfect run”; expectation is “we learn, capture gaps, and turn into concrete hardening work.”

title: Instrument canonical per-deployment throughput metrics (deployment_id/model/region) for Dedicated SLO
preview:
Context / why
We are formalizing a Dedicated throughput SLO and need a single canonical throughput signal that is attributable to a specific Dedicated deployment. Today we have a mix of per-pod / per-cluster perf counters and some request logs, but nothing that can be used as a stable SLO input for: (a) per-customer reporting (deployment_id), (b) tier-aware dashboards, and (c) SLO burn-rate alerts. This ticket adds per-deployment throughput metrics with consistent token accounting semantics (esp. streaming) and the required labels.

Scope
- Emit canonical throughput “inputs” at the inference gateway / request path for all Dedicated deployments:
  - tokens counted (input + output) with explicit definitions
  - wall clock generation time used for throughput calculations
  - request counts by completion outcome
- All metrics MUST be tagged by:
  - deployment_id (Dedicated deployment identifier; required)
  - model (canonical model slug as used in routing/catalog)
  - region (Redwood region for the serving pool)
- Provide dashboard hooks (metric names/labels/query examples) for Observability to add panels.
- Add acceptance tests and backfill documentation for metric semantics so SRE can write the SLO definition without re-litigating token accounting.

Non-goals
- This ticket does not define the final SLO thresholds or tier entitlements (handled in PM-482 / SRE SLO doc).
- This ticket does not build the full dashboards (handled by Observability). We provide stable metric contracts + sample queries.
- This ticket does not change routing behavior, admission control, or capacity policies.

Proposed metric contract (canonical)
Naming (Prometheus-style; final names can be adjusted to fit existing conventions as long as semantics match):
1) redwood_inference_generation_tokens_total
   - Type: counter
   - Value: number of tokens generated for responses (output tokens only)
   - Labels: deployment_id, model, region, streaming=(true|false)
   - Notes:
     - For streaming, increment tokens as they are flushed to the client.
     - For non-streaming, increment at request completion.

2) redwood_inference_prompt_tokens_total
   - Type: counter
   - Value: number of prompt tokens processed (input tokens)
   - Labels: deployment_id, model, region
   - Notes:
     - Includes system + user messages and any tool/function call arguments included in the prompt.

3) redwood_inference_generation_time_seconds_total
   - Type: counter
   - Value: wall-clock time attributable to token generation (seconds)
   - Labels: deployment_id, model, region, streaming=(true|false)
   - Definition:
     - Start: first token generation begins (post-queue, post-admission, after request assigned to a worker)
     - End: last generated token produced (or cancellation/timeout)
     - Excludes: time spent waiting in the front-door queue / admission control queue (we need a separate queue-time metric for saturation debugging, but SLO throughput should not be polluted by queue policy)

4) redwood_inference_requests_total
   - Type: counter
   - Value: number of completion/chat requests
   - Labels: deployment_id, model, region, status=(success|client_canceled|server_error|timeout|rate_limited|invalid_request)

Derived throughput (used by dashboards/SLO tooling)
- Output throughput (tokens/sec):
  sum(rate(redwood_inference_generation_tokens_total[5m])) / sum(rate(redwood_inference_generation_time_seconds_total[5m]))
- Optional “total tokens/sec” (prompt+output) for capacity planning:
  (sum(rate(redwood_inference_prompt_tokens_total[5m])) + sum(rate(redwood_inference_generation_tokens_total[5m]))) / sum(rate(redwood_inference_generation_time_seconds_total[5m]))

Rationale / semantics notes
- We will compute throughput from counters (tokens + time) instead of directly emitting tokens/sec as a gauge to avoid per-instance aggregation artifacts.
- We separate generation time from queue time so that overload protection changes do not retroactively change throughput measurement.
- We keep streaming=(true|false) to debug differences, but the canonical SLO query should aggregate both unless SRE decides otherwise.

Cardinality / performance constraints
- deployment_id is high cardinality but bounded by Dedicated footprint and required for customer attribution.
- model and region are low-ish cardinality.
- Do NOT add request_id or customer_id labels.
- Ensure metrics are emitted once per request per component (avoid double-counting across gateway + worker).

Implementation plan
1) Instrumentation location
- Emit counters in the request lifecycle code path where we already have:
  - resolved deployment_id (Dedicated only)
  - resolved model slug (post-routing)
  - region (serving pool region)
  - token counts from tokenizer + runtime
- For streaming, tokens are often counted at both “generated” and “sent” layers. We must choose one canonical point.
  - Decision: count output tokens at the flush-to-client boundary to reflect what the customer receives and to align with billing token accounting.

2) Token accounting sources
- Prompt tokens: from request normalization/tokenization stage.
- Output tokens: from streaming chunk encoder / final response serialization (single source of truth).
- Generation time: measured around the model execution phase (post-admission). Use monotonic clock.

3) Dedicated vs Hosted behavior
- Emit metrics only when deployment_id is present (Dedicated deployments). No-op for hosted multi-tenant routes for now to keep cardinality manageable.
- If we later need hosted per-route metrics, we’ll do that in a separate ticket with sampling and different labels.

4) Dashboard hooks
- Provide a short “how to query” section to Sergio/Allison for Grafana panels:
  - throughput floor line
  - throughput observed (p50/p95 across 5m windows)
  - saturation supporting signals: queue time, admission rejects (if available)

Acceptance criteria
- Metrics are present in the metrics backend for a canary Dedicated deployment in us-east and eu-west.
- For a controlled load test (fixed prompt/response lengths), derived throughput matches expected within +/- 5% over a 10 minute window.
- Streaming and non-streaming requests produce the same total output token counts for identical prompts/max_tokens settings (no double-counting).
- Metrics include the labels deployment_id, model, region on every sample.
- SRE can write an SLO query that returns a single time series per deployment_id (after aggregation) without additional joins.
- Add automated tests:
  - Unit test: streaming path increments output tokens exactly once per token
  - Integration test: one request, validate counters increment (prompt tokens, output tokens, generation time, requests_total)

Test plan (details)
- Local: run gateway with a fake streaming responder emitting N chunks; assert generation_tokens_total increases by exactly N_tokens, not 2*N.
- Staging: run k6 load against a Dedicated canary (single model pinned) and compare:
  - sum(output tokens) from logs/billing events vs counter delta
  - generation_time_seconds_total monotonic increase and reasonable per-request averages
- Regression: add a golden test for a known streaming payload that previously caused token double-counting.

Operational notes
- These metrics will be used for customer-facing reporting in the Console and for alerting. Treat any semantic change as a breaking change requiring review from SRE + Observability.
- Add a short docstring block in code referencing the Dedicated throughput SLO initiative and where to find the SLO definition / ADR.

Links
- Initiative ticket: PM-482 Dedicated throughput SLO and tier definitions
- Related engineering work: ENG-19288 burn-rate alerts, ENG-19257 tier-aware admission control
- Expected PR: PR-19321 add deployment throughput metrics

Open questions
- Do we want to include caching/batching effects explicitly (e.g., cache_hit label)? Current proposal: no; too high cardinality and confusing for SLO.
- Do we need to split “generated tokens” vs “delivered tokens” for streaming disconnects? Current proposal: requests_total will capture client_canceled, but output tokens counted are only delivered tokens. Confirm with SRE if SLO should treat client cancels as excluded.

Requested reviewers
- Aisha Bello (SRE implementation)
- Sergio Costa (Observability dashboards)
- Nadia Rahman (metering/cost attribution)
